general_ui_ux_design_guidelines: This project must look like a real SaaS product for a business/data analyst portfolio. Avoid the generic 'student project' look. Focus on data density (Bento High-Density Grid Mode), precise typographic hierarchy, and severe high contrast (Archetype 4: Swiss & High-Contrast). Avoid overly rounded corners; use max 8px (rounded-md) or sharp edges. Employ the 'Technical Look' with exposed grid skeleton borders. Never center align the main app container. Always left-align dense data, labels, inputs. Generously space macro layouts (p-6, p-8) to give breathing room. Implement micro-interactions on all functional elements.

typography.rules: Headings MUST use tight letter spacing (`tracking-tight` or `tracking-tighter`). Overlines and column headers MUST be Uppercase + Tiny + Wide Spacing (`tracking-[0.2em]`). Line height should be tight (`leading-none`) for headers, loose (`leading-relaxed`) for body. Never use 'Bold' blindly; use Black (900) for impact or Light (300) for elegance.

colors.rules: Never use pure #000000 or #808080. Tint neutral greys slightly. Color is function only - 95% of UI should be monochrome. Use APCA contrast tools to ensure readability. Hover state must be a lighter hue. Focus states need highest contrast.

visual_enhancers.surfaces: Use GRID BORDERS (The Technical Look) for the dashboard. Exposed skeletons. Flat backgrounds with 1px border-border, no shadow. For insights and highlight cards, use THE TRACING BEAM (subtle animated glowing border mask) to draw attention.
visual_enhancers.textures: Subtle noise overlay (grain) on dark mode to remove digital flatness. 1px solid strokes instead of soft shadows to maintain Tactical Minimalism. Do NOT use complete transparent background in header, overlays, or any surface that has content under it.

grids_and_layouts.pattern: High Density Grid (Mode B) for dashboards. Base Grid: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4. Tight gaps (gap-4 or gap-6).
grids_and_layouts.spacing: Generous Spacing Philosophy. Tailwind scale p-6, p-8 inside cards. Massive space (py-24) for footers or landing sections.
grids_and_layouts.rules: Grid items MUST be direct children of grid containers. Ensure mobile-first responsive design. Align optical centers. Use flat rectangular cardless designs for data-heavy sections.

motion.interactions: Hover lift (-translate-y-1) on cards with shadow-lg. Phosphor icons subtle scaling on hover. Tracing beam around active AI chat cards. Staggered list row entrances.
motion.rules: Transitions on specific properties only (e.g. `transition: transform 0.2s ease, opacity 0.2s ease`), NEVER `transition:all`. Respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.

component_instructions.name: KPI Cards
component_instructions.rules: Flat, 1px border. Value in IBM Plex Mono. Subtext in IBM Plex Sans. Value sizes text-4xl font-bold tracking-tighter. Use sharp edges or max 8px radius.

component_instructions.name: Charts (Recharts)
component_instructions.rules: Accessible color palettes. Remove chart axis lines for cleaner look, keep grid lines faint. Custom tooltips matching surface colors. For line charts, use 'monotone' with subtle area fills under the curve.

component_instructions.name: Filters & Tables
component_instructions.rules: Use Shadcn Select/Dropdowns for filters. Tables must have distinct headers with uppercase wide-tracking labels. Subdued hover state on table rows. Do NOT use HTML-based components for advanced user interactions.

component_instructions.name: AI Assistant Chat
component_instructions.rules: Glassmorphism for floating chat bubbles OR sharp flat design with a distinct primary colored 'assistant' side and dark neutral 'user' side. Do not use generic AI emojis.

accessibility.contrast: Minimum 4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text. Ensure focus rings are visible (focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary).
accessibility.testing: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute in kebab-case.

3d_and_creative_libraries.name: Recharts
3d_and_creative_libraries.usage: Use for all KPI data visualization (Line Chart, Bar Chart, Pie Chart). Do NOT use 3D libraries (Three.js/D3) as this needs functional clarity (Dashboard context).

3d_and_creative_libraries.name: Phosphor Icons
3d_and_creative_libraries.usage: Use Phosphor Icons (`yarn add @phosphor-icons/react`) for a dense, high-end dashboard look. Do not use emoji characters for icons.

images.description: Abstract visualization of data analytics with graphs and charts showing dynamic growth.
Elegant minimalist home office setup featuring a laptop on a white desk with stylish shelves in the background.

media.rules: Use images sparingly in dashboard context. They are best for Landing Page hero sections and 'About Project' sections. Do NOT use placeholder text.

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the JSON structures and components to build out all 6 required pages.
Implement Shadcn UI thoroughly but override default roundness (use flat, sharp rectangular styling max `rounded-md`).
The App.css/index.css must NEVER center align the .App container globally.
For the Dark/Light Mode toggle, place it prominently in the header/nav.
Apply `data-testid` to all interactive buttons, inputs, chart wrappers, and KPI values.
Build the dashboard with pure `div` and Tailwind grids (Mode B). DO NOT over-rely on generic Shadcn `Card` for main layout frames.
Charts must use Recharts and follow the strict monochromatic/high-contrast palette scheme to ensure professional look.
You have .js files not .tsx file, so make sure to use Javascript syntax (.js, .jsx) for everything.
universal_guidelines_for_main_agent: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight. Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients. Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling. Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy. You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms. You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text. Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style. NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💹🔢🏆🥇 etc for icons. Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead. Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap. Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary. Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN. Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...) Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...}). Use `sonner` for toasts. Sonner component are located in `/app/frontend/src/components/ui/sonner.jsx`.
